authoritySource: Dr. Ahmed Hesham — OET Listening Comprehensive Rulebook (AI Training Edition). Encodes every CANDIDATE-FACING exam-UX rule from the PDF as content-as-code. The profession is set to 'medicine' as a sentinel because R01.10 makes Listening profession-agnostic — the same exam-mode rules apply to every candidate. The companion authoring rulebook lives at rulebooks/listening/{profession}/rulebook.v1.json.

rules.title: Listening is sub-test 1
rules.body: Listening is the first sub-test in the OET exam. The sequence is: Listening → Reading → Writing → Speaking.

rules.title: Speaking schedule
rules.body: Speaking may be scheduled on the same day or a different day depending on the candidate's individual timetable.

rules.title: 42 questions, 24/6/12 split
rules.body: Total questions: 42. Three parts: Part A (24 marks), Part B (6 marks), Part C (12 marks).

rules.title: Part A split A1/A2
rules.body: Part A is divided into A1 (12 questions) and A2 (12 questions).

rules.title: Part C split C1/C2
rules.body: Part C is divided into C1 (6 questions) and C2 (6 questions).

rules.title: Question types
rules.body: Part A = note completion (candidate writes missing words). Parts B and C = multiple choice (3 options: A, B, C).

rules.title: Audio plays once only
rules.body: The audio plays once only throughout the entire exam. It cannot be paused, rewound, or replayed at any point during the exam.

rules.title: Total audio duration
rules.body: Total audio duration: approximately 45–50 minutes. The official timetable may show 60 minutes to include introductions and transition times.

rules.title: Listening & Reading same CBT session
rules.body: The Listening and Reading sub-tests are taken in the same session with no formal break between them in the computer-based exam.

rules.title: Profession-agnostic format
rules.body: Listening applies the same format and content for ALL OET professions. Only Writing and Speaking are profession-specific.

rules.title: 30/42 = Grade B (pass)
rules.body: 30 correct answers out of 42 = Grade B = 350 points = minimum passing score for doctors (GMC, ECFMG pathways) and nurses (NMC).

rules.title: Scoring scale
rules.body: Scoring scale: Grade A = 450+, Grade B = 350–449, Grade C+ = 300–349, Grade C = 250–299, Grade D = below 250.

rules.title: Mark distribution
rules.body: Part A carries 24 marks; Part B carries 6 marks; Part C carries 12 marks. Total = 42 marks.

rules.title: Part A spelling: whole word wrong
rules.body: Part A Note Completion — Spelling: any spelling error in a word = the entire word is marked wrong. There is no partial credit.

rules.title: Part A: minor variants accepted
rules.body: Part A Note Completion — Minor variations: minor spelling errors that do not change meaning or pronunciation may be accepted at the examiner's discretion. This is less strict than Reading Part A.

rules.title: MCQ: one correct, no negative marking
rules.body: Multiple choice questions (Parts B and C): each question has exactly one correct answer. There is no negative marking.

rules.title: No score clipping for Medicine
rules.body: No combining (clipping) of scores across exam sittings for Medicine pathways (GMC, ECFMG, Canada). All four sub-tests must be passed in a single sitting.

rules.title: Nursing (NMC) score combining
rules.body: Some nursing pathways (NMC) permit combining sub-test scores across sittings. This does not apply to Medicine.

rules.title: Part A = two consultation extracts
rules.body: Part A consists of two extracts: A1 and A2. Each extract is a consultation between a healthcare professional and a patient.

rules.title: A1 = 12 q (Q1–12), A2 = 12 q (Q13–24)
rules.body: A1 carries 12 questions (Q1–12). A2 carries 12 questions (Q13–24). Both are note completion format.

rules.title: Each Part A extract ~5 min
rules.body: Each extract audio is approximately 5 minutes long.

rules.title: Pre-A1 narrator intro + read time
rules.body: Before Extract A1 begins: a narrator introduction plays for approximately 1 minute 20 seconds, during which candidates have approximately 30 seconds to read the A1 questions.

rules.title: A1 → A2 review window
rules.body: After Extract A1 audio ends: approximately 60–90 seconds of review time are given before Extract A2 begins.

rules.title: Pre-A2 read window
rules.body: Before Extract A2 begins: approximately 30 seconds are given for candidates to read the A2 questions.

rules.title: A2 → Part B review window
rules.body: After Extract A2 audio ends: approximately 60–90 seconds of review time are given before Part B begins.

rules.title: Paper-based: ~10–13 min Listening→Reading
rules.body: In the paper-based exam: a transition of approximately 10–13 minutes occurs between Listening and Reading while papers are collected and redistributed.

rules.title: Part B = 6 independent extracts
rules.body: Part B consists of 6 questions. Each question is based on a separate, independent short audio extract.

rules.title: Part B extract ~40 s
rules.body: Each Part B audio is approximately 40 seconds long.

rules.title: Part B pre-extract context + 15 s
rules.body: Before each Part B audio: a context statement is read aloud (e.g., 'You hear a nurse briefing her colleague about a patient'). 15 seconds are given to read the question and answer options.

rules.title: Part B = 3 options, exactly one correct
rules.body: Each Part B question has 3 answer choices (A, B, C). Exactly one is correct.

rules.title: Part B settings catalogue
rules.body: Part B settings include workplace healthcare interactions: nurse briefing a colleague, doctor-patient conversation, senior-junior handover, pharmacist, physiotherapist, and similar scenarios.

rules.title: CBT Part B: one question per screen
rules.body: In the computer-based exam: one Part B question is displayed on screen at a time. The candidate must press Next to proceed to the following question.

rules.title: Paper Part B: all 6 visible
rules.body: In the paper-based exam: all 6 Part B questions are visible simultaneously on the printed page.

rules.title: Part A → Part B transition ~40 s
rules.body: Transition time between the end of Part A and the beginning of Part B: approximately 40 seconds.

rules.title: Part C = 2 long extracts (12 marks)
rules.body: Part C consists of two extracts: C1 and C2. Each carries 6 MCQ questions. Total Part C = 12 marks.

rules.title: Each Part C extract ~5 min
rules.body: Each Part C audio is a long interview or presentation on a healthcare topic, approximately 5 minutes long.

rules.title: Part C = 3 options, one correct
rules.body: Each Part C question has 3 answer choices (A, B, C). Exactly one is correct.

rules.title: Part C audio is continuous
rules.body: The audio does not pause between questions. The interview or presentation continues uninterrupted while the candidate reads questions and selects answers.

rules.title: No spoken question transitions
rules.body: The interviewer or presenter does NOT signal transitions between questions. The candidate must track progression by following the audio content.

rules.title: Part B → C1 transition ~60 s
rules.body: Transition time between the end of Part B and the beginning of C1: approximately 60 seconds.

rules.title: C1 → C2 review window ~30 s
rules.body: After C1 audio ends: approximately 30 seconds of review time are given before C2 begins.

rules.title: Pre-C2 read window ~60 s
rules.body: Before C2 audio begins: approximately 60 seconds are given to read the C2 questions.

rules.title: Final 2-min review is C2-only
rules.body: After C2 audio ends: approximately 2 minutes of review time are given for C2 only. This is the final review window of the entire Listening exam.

rules.title: Final review cannot return to C1/B/A
rules.body: The 2-minute review at the end of the exam applies to C2 only. It does not permit returning to C1, Part B, or Part A.

rules.title: CBT one-way section locks
rules.body: In the computer-based exam, once a candidate confirms moving forward from any section, returning to that section is permanently impossible for the remainder of the exam.

rules.title: A1 → A2 lock
rules.body: After the A1 review time and Next is confirmed, returning to A1 is permanently impossible.

rules.title: A2 → Part B lock
rules.body: After the A2 review time and Next is confirmed, returning to A1 or A2 is permanently impossible.

rules.title: Part B → Part C lock
rules.body: After the final Part B question and Next is confirmed, returning to Part A or Part B is permanently impossible.

rules.title: Within Part B: per-question lock
rules.body: Within Part B: each question is on a separate screen. Once Next is pressed after a question, returning to that question is impossible.

rules.title: C1 → C2 lock
rules.body: After the C1 review time and Next is confirmed, returning to C1 is permanently impossible.

rules.title: End of C2: exam closed
rules.body: After the 2-minute C2 review and Next is confirmed, the Listening exam is permanently closed. No part can be accessed again.

rules.title: Within C1: free navigation between Qs
rules.body: Within C1 during its audio: the candidate can navigate freely between all 6 C1 questions using the left-side tabs.

rules.title: Within C2: free navigation between Qs
rules.body: Within C2 during its audio: the candidate can navigate freely between all 6 C2 questions using the left-side tabs.

rules.title: Section-boundary confirmation dialog
rules.body: Every time a section boundary is crossed, the system displays a confirmation dialog: 'Are you sure you want to finish this section?' Pressing Yes is irreversible.

rules.title: Unanswered-question warning
rules.body: If a question has not been answered when Next is pressed, the system warns: 'You have not attempted this question.' The candidate may choose to answer or proceed.

rules.title: Part C layout: audio left, questions right
rules.body: In the computer-based exam, the audio for Part C appears on the LEFT panel of the screen. The questions and answer choices appear on the RIGHT panel.

rules.title: Paper: free navigation throughout
rules.body: In the paper-based exam, candidates can view any question in any part at any time while the audio is playing. There are no section locks or boundaries.

rules.title: Paper: final 2-min covers ALL parts
rules.body: At the very end of the entire paper-based Listening exam, candidates receive approximately 2 minutes to review answers across ALL parts — A, B, and C.

rules.title: Paper final 2 min: all parts editable
rules.body: These 2 final minutes in the paper-based exam allow modifying answers in Part A, Part B, or Part C freely — unlike the computer-based exam where only C2 can be reviewed at the end.

rules.title: Paper Listening→Reading transition
rules.body: Paper-based transition between Listening and Reading: approximately 10–13 minutes (papers collected, new papers distributed, candidates fill in personal details).

rules.title: Paper: all questions in one booklet
rules.body: In the paper-based exam, Part A, B, and C question papers are all accessible to the candidate simultaneously within the printed booklet.

rules.title: Part A: NO highlighting in CBT
rules.body: Part A — Highlighting: NO highlighting is available in Part A. Candidates cannot mark, underline, or annotate anything in Part A in the computer-based exam.

rules.title: Part A: typed answers, Tab navigates
rules.body: Part A — Interaction: candidates type answers directly into answer boxes. The Tab key moves the cursor between answer boxes.

rules.title: B/C: question-stem highlighting allowed
rules.body: Parts B and C — Highlighting the question stem: permitted. The candidate selects text in the question to highlight it yellow. Clicking again removes the highlight.

rules.title: B/C: answer choices CANNOT be highlighted
rules.body: Parts B and C — Highlighting the answer choices (A, B, C): NOT permitted. No marking tool is available on the answer options themselves.

rules.title: B/C: right-click strikethrough on choices
rules.body: Parts B and C — Eliminating an answer choice: the candidate right-clicks on an answer option to apply a strikethrough mark. This eliminates it without selecting it as the answer.

rules.title: B/C: left-click selects answer
rules.body: Parts B and C — Selecting the correct answer: the candidate left-clicks on the chosen answer. It highlights in a selection colour, confirming it as the chosen answer. Only one answer can be selected per question.

rules.title: Zoom: app +/− buttons, Ctrl+ blocked
rules.body: Zoom: the exam application provides + and − buttons for zoom in/out. The browser keyboard shortcut Ctrl+ is blocked within the exam environment.

rules.title: Part C audio panel stays visible
rules.body: Part C audio panel: the audio control panel must remain visible on the left side of the screen at all times. If it launches as a pop-up, it must be repositioned to show both panels simultaneously.

rules.title: Paper: pencil annotations everywhere
rules.body: In the paper-based exam, candidates may highlight, underline, circle, or make any pencil marks on the question paper — in Part A, B, or C — without restriction.

rules.title: Paper: separate answer sheet
rules.body: Part A answers are written directly on the answer sheet. The question booklet is separate and may be annotated freely.

rules.title: Paper: pencil only, no digital tools
rules.body: In the paper-based exam, there are no digital tools. All annotation is made physically with a pencil on the printed paper.

rules.title: Min screen resolution 1920×1080
rules.body: Minimum screen resolution: 1920 × 1080. Resolutions below this cause display problems — text may be cut off or panels may overlap.

rules.title: Display scale ≤ 125%
rules.body: Display scale (Windows): must be set to 100% or maximum 125%. Scale above 125% causes the exam interface to clip and overlap.

rules.title: Wired headset / earphones REQUIRED
rules.body: Audio device: a wired headset or earphones are required for the Listening sub-test. Bluetooth audio devices are not permitted.

rules.title: Volume: candidate-controlled
rules.body: Volume adjustment: the candidate controls their own volume level. The exam system does not control this. Volume is adjusted by the candidate at the start of the exam.

rules.title: Stable broadband required
rules.body: Internet connection: a stable broadband connection is required throughout the exam.

rules.title: VPN / VM must be off
rules.body: All VPNs and virtual machines must be disabled before the exam begins.

rules.title: ProProctor dedicated app
rules.body: The exam runs inside a dedicated application (ProProctor), not a regular browser. The application locks the device during the exam session.

rules.title: Test-centre wired audio + candidate volume
rules.body: At test centres: audio is delivered through a wired headset or earphone socket at the candidate's workstation. The candidate adjusts the volume level to their preference.
